content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Resolved all 3 pending amendments from round-8 review for the success reconciliation handler (bead 9ni.8.5.1). Amendment 3 (lease retention, highest priority): Both mark_failed-failure handlers (multi-repo at daemon_loop.rs ~939, single-repo at ~1505) now return immediately WITHOUT releasing the lease when mark_failed itself fails. Previously the lease was released, which stranded the task in Active/Claimed with no scanner (Phase 3 only scans Pending, Phase 0 only scans label_dirty) and no recovery path. Retaining the lease preserves the `daemon reconcile` stale-lease recovery path: LeaseService::reconcile (lease_service.rs:462-492) scans all leases for expired heartbeats and marks Claimed/Active tasks as Failed with class reconciliation_timeout. The branch is still pushed before returning so checkpoint work is preserved. Amendment 1 (recovery documentation): Added detailed comments at both mark_failed-failure sites explaining: (1) the lease is intentionally retained, (2) Phase 3/Phase 0 scanner limitations that would strand the task if the lease were released, (3) `daemon reconcile` detects stale leases and transitions tasks to Failed, (4) persistent store corruption requires operator intervention via `daemon reconcile` or manual store repair. Amendment 2 (milestone state divergence): Enhanced the MilestoneUpdateFailed arm comment (daemon_loop.rs ~1792-1808) with a KNOWN DIVERGENCE section documenting that milestone snapshot progress may be stale while the bead is closed in br and the task is completed, since mark_completed prevents reprocessing. Added TODO(9ni.8.6 or later) suggesting a Phase 0 milestone-repair pass that detects closed beads (via `br show`) with missing completion records in the milestone snapshot and backfills record_bead_completion.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 3: Changed both mark_failed-failure handlers to return immediately without releasing the lease. Multi-repo (daemon_loop.rs ~939): the Err arm now calls try_push_failed_task_branch and returns Ok(()) without calling release_task_lease. Single-repo (daemon_loop.rs ~1505): same pattern. This preserves the `daemon reconcile` stale-lease recovery path — LeaseService::reconcile at lease_service.rs:462-492 scans for expired heartbeats and marks Claimed/Active tasks Failed with reconciliation_timeout class
2. [Completed] Amendment 1: Added detailed documentation comments at both mark_failed-failure sites explaining: the lease is intentionally retained because Phase 3 only scans Pending and Phase 0 only scans label_dirty; releasing would strand the task permanently; `daemon reconcile` detects stale leases; persistent store corruption requires operator intervention
3. [Completed] Amendment 2: Enhanced MilestoneUpdateFailed arm comment (daemon_loop.rs ~1792-1808) with KNOWN DIVERGENCE section documenting stale milestone progress after mark_completed, and TODO(9ni.8.6 or later) suggesting a Phase 0 milestone-repair pass that detects closed beads with missing completion records via `br show` and backfills record_bead_completion
4. [Completed] Ran cargo test (757 total: 756 passed + 1 pre-existing flaky checkpoint_test that passes in isolation), cargo clippy (0 new warnings), cargo fmt --check (clean). All 9 success_reconciliation tests pass including end-to-end

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 756 passed, 1 failed (pre-existing flaky checkpoint_test::worktree_adapter_creates_finds_and_resets_checkpoint_commits — passes when run individually, fails only under parallel test execution due to git worktree interference, documented since round 2)
- cargo test contexts::automation_runtime::success_reconciliation: 9 passed (8 unit + 1 end-to-end), 0 failed
- cargo fmt --check: no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: 0 new warnings in modified files (daemon_loop.rs)
- Amendment 3 verified: multi-repo mark_failed Err arm at ~939 returns Ok(()) without calling release_task_lease; single-repo mark_failed Err arm at ~1505 returns Ok(()) without calling release_task_lease. Both still call try_push_failed_task_branch before returning to preserve checkpoint commits
- Amendment 3 recovery path verified: LeaseService::reconcile at lease_service.rs:462-492 scans all leases, checks is_stale_at(now), and for Claimed/Active tasks calls mark_failed with class reconciliation_timeout — confirming automated recovery via operator-invoked `daemon reconcile`
- Amendment 1 verified: detailed comments at both sites document lease retention rationale, Phase 3/Phase 0 scanner limitations, daemon reconcile recovery path, and persistent failure escalation to operator
- Amendment 2 verified: MilestoneUpdateFailed arm at ~1792-1808 includes KNOWN DIVERGENCE section documenting stale milestone progress and TODO(9ni.8.6) for Phase 0 milestone-repair pass
- End-to-end test reconcile_success_end_to_end_updates_milestone continues to pass with all assertions including structural task_id and hint persistence

## Outstanding Risks

- The lineage task_id extraction still parses the 'task_id=<value>' format from outcome_detail via the extract_task_id_from_outcome_detail helper. This couples the fs adapter to a format defined in success_reconciliation.rs. A future refactor should thread task_id as a first-class parameter through the record_bead_completion/update_task_run chain to eliminate this format dependency.
- The next_step_hint.json file is overwritten on each successful reconciliation. If multiple beads complete in quick succession for the same milestone, only the last hint survives. Acceptable since hints are informational and read once by the selection step.
- When mark_failed fails and the lease is retained (amendment 3), the task is stuck until an operator runs `daemon reconcile`. The daemon loop itself does NOT call LeaseService::reconcile automatically, so recovery requires manual intervention. If the store corruption is permanent, `daemon reconcile` will also fail at mark_failed (it calls the same code path at lease_service.rs:484-490) and the operator must repair the store file directly.
- The MilestoneUpdateFailed milestone state divergence (amendment 2) has no automated repair until TODO(9ni.8.6) is implemented. Operators must manually reconcile milestone snapshot progress when they see the WARNING log. The bead is correctly closed in br, so `br show` can confirm the true state.
